reviews.review_msg: Dr. Leith was amazing, really sweet, approachable, and engaging (even though the content gets boring at times). Make sure to do your readings in advance, go over it two or three times and you'll thank yourself when the final assignment comes around. Participate in the tutorials, talk to your TA, it'll help you (shoutout to Ben)!

A lot depends on your TA. Mine was simply great, not sure about the others, but i think they were too. Honestly nothing much to say about Leith herself, as she simplified things to make it accessible to the broadest audience possible. If you are new to literally analysis, just make sure to go to office hours (both TA and Leith) and you'll get it.

So passionate about her work so she's expressive whilst explaining. Sometimes her concepts can be confusing as in she thinks we are also as passionate as she is so she talks to us like we are already at a certain level. Tutorials are great, pray you get a good TA and you'll ace the essays and final.

She was very caring to all the students in the class while also being very passionate about this course subject. The material was pretty dry and it felt like a history course, but she made the class fun for us. A lot of reading expected for every week.

Dr. Davis was welcoming, kind, and excellent at explaining concepts. We had a 7-pg term paper, a midterm, and a final. Texts were a little dry, but she elaborated on any misunderstandings. Participation matters for tutorials and is encouraged for lectures, but she doesn't force you. Lectures are heavy and not recorded, but PPs are uploaded.

Huge attitude problem, do not expect any useful feedback. If she decides she has a negative opinion of your work she will treat you poorly to your face, she will not answer clarifying questions, yet she will demand respect when being spoken to. Truly a disgusting woman.

Leith was super sweet. Our lectures were online, but tutorials were in person. Make sure to do the readings as you need it for tutorial participation and for end of lecture paragraphs. As a non-english major, she was such an awesome prof and I hope to have her again one day.

Although the course material was quite dry a lot of the time, she did a good job trying to keep us engaged. There were only two graded assignments and the final exam. The rest of our grade was determined by our participation in tutorials. Overall the assignments weren't too difficult. She was very approachable if any clarification was needed.
English with her felt pointless. I did learn a few things but overall I felt like I was wasting my time. Good prof, but the topics were not engaging

Professor Davis goes above an beyond to make sure a question gets answered and she's always so nice and respectful. The readings and lectures can get a bit heavy at times but overall, a good class. There are a few really long, boring chapters though.

Absolute joke of a class. She is stuck in the past. She is so fixed on us knowing how to cite everything by memory. She does not realise that internet exists now. Her finals expectation is insane for a 100 level class. Extremely strict just to fit her agenda. I am sure a lot of year 1 students are scarred by her. DW, its just her.

My second time having her as a prof, and Leith is great. She provides an interesting, and inspirational spin on Romanticism that makes lectures worthwhile. Her reading list can be heavy at times, but its expected for an upper-year English course. Lastly, she is very approachable outside of class and will do the most in order for you to succeed!

Dr. Davis seems very engaged in her lectures and is very caring of her students. She answers questions asked during class and tries her best to explain it to the rest of the class. The reading in this course is a bit heavy but not impossible.

She's one of the best professors I had at SFU. She is a tough marker, but she gave every possible help to me in order for me to succeed in my writing. She's wonderful, and the topics were very interesting and gave more perspectives to English literature that I have not thought of.

The best English professor at SFU! She is always willing to help her students and genuinely wants to see them succeed. She is passionate about the material that she teaches, and she creates a safe learning environment for all students. She provides detailed feedback on all your assignments, so you know how to improve.

Although  the subject matter dragged, the class was not bad. She is engaging. Lots of assignments for an upper level English class, with a group project, but easy grader. If you are looking for a prof that'll give you marks for  taking a few risks, here's the one. She's incredibly nice as well.

She's really kind and caring, I would definitely recommend her. Always available and willing to help. Thankfully also not monotone like many English Profs! She also knows how to really get you engaged with the material and even brings in guests :)

If you consider showing up for a class you paid for and typing 250 words a week for free participation marks difficult, you should probably drop out. Leith is one of those rare professors that actually cares about her students. She provides very detailed feedback on essays/exams, and tries her best to make the class fun for everybody.

She really wants you to participate and the more you participate the more you start enjoying the course. Davis is very caring and gives amazing feedback. There are online discussions along with group discussions but because the class is smaller it's very enjoyable. I would take her again as she's open to differing opinions and encourages it!

Professor Davis expects literally everybody in this mandatory survey course to care for Romantic Lit like it was their own child. She expects every student to not only attend mandatory morning tutorials, but also produce discussion comments--"substantial reflections rather than subjective responses"--once a week. Keeners: go for it. Else, beware.

I love her! She is one of my favourite professors at SFU! I've actually had her for two classes now and am looking at taking another one with her next semester. She is really nice and she is really helpful. She tries so hard to make it interesting for students and she gives great feedback.

Dr. Leith is an erudite professor. She offers a unique course & does her best to make dry authors' works an interesting read. She provides excellent class notes on the material & is a strong lecturer. Overall, she is a caring professor & gives you great, detailed feedback. She is a fair marker, the grade you receive reflects the work you put in. A+

Best professor ever ! She is super understanding and kind!! She is a tough marker but she is willing to sit and help you!! She gets when you are having a tough day or a tough semester. I was having family and work problems and she was more than generous to me. Questioning if I want to take another class with her but the material is a little dry :/

Very passionate about the material, provides lots of canvas help including 'translations' for more difficult texts. Takes class suggestions well and is always looking to improve student's experience. The texts assigned were mostly interesting, save a few that were provided mostly for historical context (unless that's your thing).
Best English prof I've had at SFU. Clear cut, easy midterm and final, enjoyable material and nice to listen to!

I forced myself to go to every lecture because I had hoped it would be helpful in the end, but really I regretted going every time.  Lectures are repetitive and not very helpful.  If you have a good TA, go to your tutorials, and read all the books, you'll be fine.  However, I would not take this class again.

Leith is one of the best profs I've had at SFU. She tries so hard to ensure that you can have a one-on-one with her after the first essay to improve on your second. The feedback on my first essay was a page long and printed. I've been in university for three years and this is the first time I've had a prof care that much.
It was a great class. She really enjoys and understands the material and she is really helpful if you have any trouble.

This prof is my favourite so far at SFU. Her lectures were interesting and engaging, she was funny and brought props to help people remember specific events, organized a live performance and overall rocked. She also gave sample exams and potential exam questions ahead of time and assigned minimal reading, no reason not to do well in this class.

I love her! She is so excited about the subject and tries really hard to get class engaged. She brings props, puts on videos and brought in singers. She is always willing to help if you go see her during office hours and she gives mock exams before midterm and final to help you study.
AWESOME
Leith's powerpoints are very informative, she loves her student and I never skipped her classes cuz it was never boring.
Leith seems like a cool person and she is quite nice and can give good input. However, the material was often boring! I found analyzing novels much easier than this.

Very passionate about what she's teaching. If you need extra help  she is approachable and available. Obviously the class is going to be a struggle if you're not a great writer. My TA was not the easiest marker- although its hard to say since we've only gotten back one assignment at this point.

If your taking this to get a writing credit, take something else. she doesnt teach anything. you read essays between class and talk about them during class, then your handed an assignment with no guidance on how to do it the way she wants it. Could be a real easy class if your a natural great writer. If not, take another class for the W credit

Leith loves her students and is extremely passionate about teaching. Her lectures are informative and interesting, and the seminars were equally engaging. She gives excellent feedback and is the sweetest professor you will ever have.

Horrible professor. Breaking up the large lecture hall into groups for discussions useless to the text and then not even asking about conclusions. An entire class dedicated to some folk singers. This isn't high school I'm paying for this! Completely unclear as to guidelines for papers.

A very engaging prof. I'd say one of the best english teachers in SFU with her clairity and overall interest in her job and students. I liked how she encouraged questions and thoughts in class. Notes were well organized - not too fast and not too much. The way she talks makes me giggle a bit though.

She really cares about her student's success in class. Consider yourself lucky for Leith, there are a lot worse professors at SFU that pale in comparison to Leith. Save your complaints for the upper level engl profs. She's not a hard marker either.

Struggled at the time w/ the course's focus on secondary critical readings (some of which were very challenging), but in retrospect appreciated the push.Her lectures were clear and organized, and what they may have occasionally lacked in spark was more than compensated by their thoroughness.Essay feedback was similarly detailed & thoughtful, & chal
The only thing memorable about her class was her cool first name.

Very average. Nothing horrible, but then nothing stellar either. Had some neat insights into the material, but come exam time all that really mattered was your own take on it, making the lectures pretty much pointless.

She was a terrible prof, her classes filled with useless filler and crap...Everything taught was useless for the essays or the massive essay final exams....No help for what was expected of me...Everyone i knew skipped about half the time, I stayed there and was bored out of my skull.  NEVER AGAIN!!!
Dr. Davis really cares bout her students.

Good book selection, but not enough time spent on each, I felt. Fair marker. I didn't really feel as though going to lectures was really necessary, at least from a marking perspective. I was surprised that this course wasn't just a recap of grade ten english. ended up learning some new things.
meh alright, but repetitive really really repeittive
She was by far one of the best profs I've had at SFU. Probably my favourite. She made the class very interesting and I would take any class with her in a heartbeat!
she was great :)  i liked her but she left a little bit over mid-semester :/
Her Travel Lit class was imaginative and brilliant.  She is at her best when engaging with new material (not Burns), which she does far more than most S.F.U. profs.
She is a fair marker, but overall, her lectures and tutorials were pretty boring.. The essay topics she gave were uninteresting as well.
in a word: boring
very interesting speaker... easy to listen to.
Boring, not good at discussion
Leith is a great professor. Love the accent!
